Scramble eggs, break into pieces, remove. Add oil, stir-fry garlic 15 sec. Add carrots, peas, bok choy stems, cook 2 min. Add cold rice, press flat against wok, let char 30 sec, toss. Repeat. Add soy sauce, sesame oil. Return eggs, add bok choy leaves, toss until wilted. Top with green onions.","alternatives":[{"id":"r082","name":"Biang Biang Noodles","name_zh":"油泼面","cuisine":"Chinese","cooking_method":"boil","effort_level":"long","flavor_tags":["aromatic","herby","savory","spicy"],"serves":4,"pcsv_roles":{"carb":["bread flour"],"sauce":["water","salt","neutral oil","soy sauce","oyster sauce","chinese black vinegar","szechuan peppercorns","chinese red chili flakes"],"veggie":["fresh ginger","napa cabbage","scallions","chinese chives","chinese celery","garlic"]},"ingredients_have":[],"ingredients_need":["bread flour","water","salt","neutral oil","soy sauce","oyster sauce","chinese black vinegar","fresh ginger","napa cabbage","scallions","chinese chives","chinese celery","garlic","szechuan peppercorns","chinese red chili flakes"],"ingredients":[{"name":"bread flour","amount":"400 g","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"water","amount":"200 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"salt","amount":"8 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"neutral oil","amount":"to prevent sticking","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"soy sauce","amount":"60 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"oyster sauce","amount":"20 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"chinese black vinegar","amount":"20 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"fresh ginger","amount":"a small knob","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"napa cabbage","amount":"a handful","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"scallions","amount":"1 stalk","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"chinese chives","amount":"1-2","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"chinese celery","amount":"1/2 stalk","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"garlic","amount":"2-4 cloves","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"szechuan peppercorns","amount":"a spoonful","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"chinese red chili flakes","amount":"a spoonful","pcsv":["sauce"]}],"instructions":"Step 1: Start the noodle dough (in advance)\nSet a bowl over a scale. Add the flour, water, and salt. Mix the dough with your hands until a ball is formed. The dough will be fairly stiff and tough, so knead for about 5 minutes in the bowl. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let rest for 30 minutes.\n\nStep 2: Finish the noodle dough (in advance)\nThe dough should feel much more elastic and supple after resting. Once rested, knead the dough for another 3 to 5 minutes, then rest for 5 more minutes. Using a rolling pin and your hands, work the dough into a rectangle about 6\" by 12\" (15 cm by 30 cm). Cut the dough into six (100 g) pieces, making them each about 2\" (5 cm) wide. Brush the noodle pieces with a neutral oil. Add a drizzle to a container and stack noodles slightly overlapping like leaning books, not right on top of each other. Cover the container and let rest for at least 1 hour or in the fridge for up to 3 days.\n\nStep 3: Prep the sauce & aromatics\nWhile the dough rests, do some prep work. In a bowl or container, mix together all noodle sauce ingredients until well combined. Cover and set aside. Chop or gather any remaining vegetables or aromatics. Set aside.\n\nStep 4: Pull the noodles\nIf your noodles have been resting in the fridge, pull them out to warm up on the counter for ~10 minutes. When you are ready to finish the dish, you can proceed with pulling the noodles. Since this will take some time to do, bring a large pot of water to boil while you pull the noodles. Using a rolling pin, roll the dough piece about double the current width and 1 inch longer. Using a chopstick or back of a knife, gently imprint the center of the noodle to give it a 'perforated' ripping point. Grab the ends of the rectangle with your thumbs and forefingers and pull the dough gently, stretching until about shoulder length. Now the fun part. While still slightly pulling, bounce the noodle flat against the counter in an up and down motion until the dough is about 4 feet (1.25 m) long. This takes a little practice. Do not grip too tight or pull too quickly or the noodle will break. If it does, just pull the thick part where it broke. When the noodle is 4 ft, pick it up at the middle and rip it down the line. Pull until you reach the bottom, but don't pull all the way through, leaving you with a large noodle ring. Set aside and repeat the process.\n\nStep 5: Boil the noodles & blanch the cabbage\nOnce your noodles are pulled and your large pot is boiling, you can cook the noodles. Add the noodles to the boiling water and cook them until they float, which should take around 2 minutes. During the last 30 seconds of boiling, add the cabbage into the water with the noodles to cook through. If you are working with a smaller pot, it is ok to do this in batches. Drain the noodles and cabbage and set aside into a large heatproof bowl for saucing.\n\nStep 6: Sauce the noodles\nHeat the oil in a small pot or skillet until it is hot but not yet smoking (~375°F/190°C). To the bowl of cooked noodles and blanched cabbage, pour the sauce over top. Then add all remaining aromatics and spices in a pile on top of the noodles. Drizzle the hot oil right on the aromatics, making sure everything sizzles and blooms from its heat. Toss the noodles until everything is evenly coated, taste and adjust the seasoning if needed, and serve right away while the noodles are still warm.","alternatives":[]},{"id":"r089","name":"Pasta Alla Checca","name_zh":"鲜番茄冷拌意面","cuisine":"Italian","cooking_method":"boil","effort_level":"quick","flavor_tags":["herby","savory","smoky"],"serves":4,"pcsv_roles":{"carb":["spaghetti"],"sauce":["water","salt","olive oil","black pepper"],"veggie":["tomatoes","fresh basil","fresh oregano","fresh thyme"],"protein":["fresh mozzarella"]},"ingredients_have":[],"ingredients_need":["spaghetti","water","salt","tomatoes","fresh mozzarella","olive oil","fresh basil","fresh oregano","fresh thyme","black pepper"],"ingredients":[{"name":"spaghetti","amount":"227 g (1/2 lb)","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"water","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"salt","amount":"a sprinkle","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"tomatoes","amount":"454 g (1 lb)","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"fresh mozzarella","amount":"113 g (1/4 lb)","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"olive oil","amount":"45 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"fresh basil","amount":"10 leaves, torn","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"fresh oregano","amount":"a spoonful, chopped","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"fresh thyme","amount":"a spoonful, chopped","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"black pepper","amount":"a sprinkle","pcsv":["sauce"]}],"instructions":"Step 1: Boil the noodles\nSet a pot of water to boil along with a generous sprinkle of salt. Once boiling, add pasta and cook until al dente, or 1 minute less than package instructions recommend.\n\nStep 2: Prepare the sauce\nChop tomatoes into small pieces and add to serving bowl. Sprinkle salt over tomatoes, add oregano and thyme, and mix. Add olive oil to a small pan over medium heat. After a few minutes, pour off excess tomato liquid. Add torn basil leaves and black pepper. Remove oil from heat as soon as it starts to smoke. Pour hot olive oil over ingredients to bloom flavors, then mix thoroughly.\n\nStep 3: Toss the pasta & serve\nSave pasta water before draining. Add the flour, water, and salt. Mix the dough with your hands until a ball is formed. The dough will be fairly stiff and tough, so knead for about 5 minutes in the bowl. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let rest for 30 minutes.\n\nStep 2: Finish the noodle dough (in advance)\nThe dough should feel much more elastic and supple after resting. Once rested, knead the dough for another 3 to 5 minutes, then rest for 5 more minutes. Using a rolling pin and your hands, work the dough into a rectangle about 6\" by 12\" (15 cm by 30 cm). Cut the dough into six (100 g) pieces, making them each about 2\" (5 cm) wide. Brush the noodle pieces with a neutral oil. Add a drizzle to a container and stack noodles slightly overlapping like leaning books, not right on top of each other. Cover the container and let rest for at least 1 hour or in the fridge for up to 3 days.\n\nStep 3: Prep the sauce & aromatics\nWhile the dough rests, do some prep work. In a bowl or container, mix together all noodle sauce ingredients until well combined. Cover and set aside. Chop or gather any remaining vegetables or aromatics. Set aside.\n\nStep 4: Pull the noodles\nIf your noodles have been resting in the fridge, pull them out to warm up on the counter for ~10 minutes. When you are ready to finish the dish, you can proceed with pulling the noodles. Since this will take some time to do, bring a large pot of water to boil while you pull the noodles. Using a rolling pin, roll the dough piece about double the current width and 1 inch longer. Using a chopstick or back of a knife, gently imprint the center of the noodle to give it a 'perforated' ripping point. Grab the ends of the rectangle with your thumbs and forefingers and pull the dough gently, stretching until about shoulder length. Now the fun part. While still slightly pulling, bounce the noodle flat against the counter in an up and down motion until the dough is about 4 feet (1.25 m) long. This takes a little practice. Do not grip too tight or pull too quickly or the noodle will break. If it does, just pull the thick part where it broke. When the noodle is 4 ft, pick it up at the middle and rip it down the line. Pull until you reach the bottom, but don't pull all the way through, leaving you with a large noodle ring. Set aside and repeat the process.\n\nStep 5: Boil the noodles & blanch the cabbage\nOnce your noodles are pulled and your large pot is boiling, you can cook the noodles. Add the noodles to the boiling water and cook them until they float, which should take around 2 minutes. During the last 30 seconds of boiling, add the cabbage into the water with the noodles to cook through. If you are working with a smaller pot, it is ok to do this in batches. Drain the noodles and cabbage and set aside into a large heatproof bowl for saucing.\n\nStep 6: Sauce the noodles\nHeat the oil in a small pot or skillet until it is hot but not yet smoking (~375°F/190°C). To the bowl of cooked noodles and blanched cabbage, pour the sauce over top. Then add all remaining aromatics and spices in a pile on top of the noodles. Drizzle the hot oil right on the aromatics, making sure everything sizzles and blooms from its heat. Toss the noodles until everything is evenly coated, taste and adjust the seasoning if needed, and serve right away while the noodles are still warm.","alternatives":[]},{"id":"r089","name":"Pasta Alla Checca","name_zh":"鲜番茄冷拌意面","cuisine":"Italian","cooking_method":"boil","effort_level":"quick","flavor_tags":["herby","savory","smoky"],"serves":4,"pcsv_roles":{"carb":["spaghetti"],"sauce":["water","salt","olive oil","black pepper"],"veggie":["tomatoes","fresh basil","fresh oregano","fresh thyme"],"protein":["fresh mozzarella"]},"ingredients_have":[],"ingredients_need":["spaghetti","water","salt","tomatoes","fresh mozzarella","olive oil","fresh basil","fresh oregano","fresh thyme","black pepper"],"ingredients":[{"name":"spaghetti","amount":"227 g (1/2 lb)","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"water","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"salt","amount":"a sprinkle","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"tomatoes","amount":"454 g (1 lb)","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"fresh mozzarella","amount":"113 g (1/4 lb)","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"olive oil","amount":"45 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"fresh basil","amount":"10 leaves, torn","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"fresh oregano","amount":"a spoonful, chopped","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"fresh thyme","amount":"a spoonful, chopped","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"black pepper","amount":"a sprinkle","pcsv":["sauce"]}],"instructions":"Step 1: Boil the noodles\nSet a pot of water to boil along with a generous sprinkle of salt. Once boiling, add pasta and cook until al dente, or 1 minute less than package instructions recommend.\n\nStep 2: Prepare the sauce\nChop tomatoes into small pieces and add to serving bowl. Sprinkle salt over tomatoes, add oregano and thyme, and mix. Add olive oil to a small pan over medium heat. After a few minutes, pour off excess tomato liquid. Add torn basil leaves and black pepper. Remove oil from heat as soon as it starts to smoke. Pour hot olive oil over ingredients to bloom flavors, then mix thoroughly.\n\nStep 3: Toss the pasta & serve\nSave pasta water before draining. Drain pasta and add to tomato bowl. Toss until combined, adding pasta water if needed. Taste and adjust salt. Toss in mozzarella balls, cover bowl 1 minute to allow melting, then serve immediately.","alternatives":[]}]}}],"pcsv":{"event_type":"pcsv_update","pcsv":{"protein":{"status":"low","items":["chicken"]},"carb":{"status":"low","items":["rice"]},"veggie":{"status":"gap","items":[]},"sauce":{"status":"gap","items":[]}}},"explanation":"You’ve got a strong base, but the KB matches are mostly vegetarian, so I’d steer this toward a BBQ-style vegetarian side dinner, or you can swap in the chicken on your own grill setup.\n\nBest matches for your rice and vegetables:\n- Vegetarian Fried Rice / 素炒饭 — quick, uses your rice, adds a savory finish; source: recipe KB\n- Vegetarian Bibimbap / 素拌饭 — more of a Korean BBQ-feeling bowl, needs extra veggies and a sauce; source: recipe KB\n- Tofu and Bok Choy Stir-Fry / 豆腐炒青菜 — gives you a balanced main with rice, though it’s not BBQ-grilled; source: recipe KB\n\nPCSV check: protein low, carb low, veggie gap, sauce gap. So for a true BBQ dinner for eight, you’ll want to add a sauce and more vegetables, and likely more protein if you want everyone well fed.\n\nIf you want, I can next help you:\n- turn this into a BBQ chicken dinner shopping list for 8, or\n- adapt one of the vegetarian KB recipes into a BBQ-style meal plan.","done":{"event_type":"done","status":"complete","reason":null,"error_category":null,"token_usage":{"prompt_tokens":9679,"completion_tokens":358,"total_tokens":10037,"cached_tokens":6144,"cache_write_tokens":0,"cost":0.00472305,"model":"openai/gpt-5.4-mini-20260317"}},"dish_count":4,"status":"complete"}

Top with green onions.", "alternatives": [{"id": "r082", "name": "Biang Biang Noodles", "name_zh": "油泼面", "cuisine": "Chinese", "cooking_method": "boil", "effort_level": "long", "flavor_tags": ["aromatic", "herby", "savory", "spicy"], "serves": 4, "pcsv_roles": {"carb": ["bread flour"], "sauce": ["water", "salt", "neutral oil", "soy sauce", "oyster sauce", "chinese black vinegar", "szechuan peppercorns", "chinese red chili flakes"], "veggie": ["fresh ginger", "napa cabbage", "scallions", "chinese chives", "chinese celery", "garlic"]}, "ingredients_have": [], "ingredients_need": ["bread flour", "water", "salt", "neutral oil", "soy sauce", "oyster sauce", "chinese black vinegar", "fresh ginger", "napa cabbage", "scallions", "chinese chives", "chinese celery", "garlic", "szechuan peppercorns", "chinese red chili flakes"], "ingredients": [{"name": "bread flour", "amount": "400 g", "pcsv": ["carb"]}, {"name": "water", "amount": "200 g", "pcsv": ["sauce"]}, {"name": "salt", "amount": "8 g", "pcsv": ["sauce"]}, {"name": "neutral oil", "amount": "to prevent sticking", "pcsv": ["sauce"]}, {"name": "soy sauce", "amount": "60 g", "pcsv": ["sauce"]}, {"name": "oyster sauce", "amount": "20 g", "pcsv": ["sauce"]}, {"name": "chinese black vinegar", "amount": "20 g", "pcsv": ["sauce"]}, {"name": "fresh ginger", "amount": "a small knob", "pcsv": ["veggie"]}, {"name": "napa cabbage", "amount": "a handful", "pcsv": ["veggie"]}, {"name": "scallions", "amount": "1 stalk", "pcsv": ["veggie"]}, {"name": "chinese chives", "amount": "1-2", "pcsv": ["veggie"]}, {"name": "chinese celery", "amount": "1/2 stalk", "pcsv": ["veggie"]}, {"name": "garlic", "amount": "2-4 cloves", "pcsv": ["veggie"]}, {"name": "szechuan peppercorns", "amount": "a spoonful", "pcsv": ["sauce"]}, {"name": "chinese red chili flakes", "amount": "a spoonful", "pcsv": ["sauce"]}], "instructions": "Step 1: Start the noodle dough (in advance)
Set a bowl over a scale. Add the flour, water, and salt. Mix the dough with your hands until a ball is formed. The dough will be fairly stiff and tough, so knead for about 5 minutes in the bowl. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let rest for 30 minutes.

Step 2: Finish the noodle dough (in advance)
The dough should feel much more elastic and supple after resting. Once rested, knead the dough for another 3 to 5 minutes, then rest for 5 more minutes. Using a rolling pin and your hands, work the dough into a rectangle about 6" by 12" (15 cm by 30 cm). Cut the dough into six (100 g) pieces, making them each about 2" (5 cm) wide. Brush the noodle pieces with a neutral oil. Add a drizzle to a container and stack noodles slightly overlapping like leaning books, not right on top of each other. Cover the container and let rest for at least 1 hour or in the fridge for up to 3 days.

Step 3: Prep the sauce & aromatics
While the dough rests, do some prep work. In a bowl or container, mix together all noodle sauce ingredients until well combined. Cover and set aside. Chop or gather any remaining vegetables or aromatics. Set aside.

Step 4: Pull the noodles
If your noodles have been resting in the fridge, pull them out to warm up on the counter for ~10 minutes. When you are ready to finish the dish, you can proceed with pulling the noodles. Since this will take some time to do, bring a large pot of water to boil while you pull the noodles. Using a rolling pin, roll the dough piece about double the current width and 1 inch longer. Using a chopstick or back of a knife, gently imprint the center of the noodle to give it a 'perforated' ripping point. Grab the ends of the rectangle with your thumbs and forefingers and pull the dough gently, stretching until about shoulder length. Now the fun part. While still slightly pulling, bounce the noodle flat against the counter in an up and down motion until the dough is about 4 feet (1.25 m) long. This takes a little practice. Do not grip too tight or pull too quickly or the noodle will break. If it does, just pull the thick part where it broke. When the noodle is 4 ft, pick it up at the middle and rip it down the line. Pull until you reach the bottom, but don't pull all the way through, leaving you with a large noodle ring. Set aside and repeat the process.

Step 5: Boil the noodles & blanch the cabbage
Once your noodles are pulled and your large pot is boiling, you can cook the noodles. Add the noodles to the boiling water and cook them until they float, which should take around 2 minutes. During the last 30 seconds of boiling, add the cabbage into the water with the noodles to cook through. If you are working with a smaller pot, it is ok to do this in batches. Drain the noodles and cabbage and set aside into a large heatproof bowl for saucing.

Step 6: Sauce the noodles
Heat the oil in a small pot or skillet until it is hot but not yet smoking (~375°F/190°C). To the bowl of cooked noodles and blanched cabbage, pour the sauce over top. Then add all remaining aromatics and spices in a pile on top of the noodles. Drizzle the hot oil right on the aromatics, making sure everything sizzles and blooms from its heat. Step 2: Finish the noodle dough (in advance)
The dough should feel much more elastic and supple after resting. Once rested, knead the dough for another 3 to 5 minutes, then rest for 5 more minutes. Using a rolling pin and your hands, work the dough into a rectangle about 6" by 12" (15 cm by 30 cm). Cut the dough into six (100 g) pieces, making them each about 2" (5 cm) wide. Brush the noodle pieces with a neutral oil. Add a drizzle to a container and stack noodles slightly overlapping like leaning books, not right on top of each other. Cover the container and let rest for at least 1 hour or in the fridge for up to 3 days.

Step 3: Prep the sauce & aromatics
While the dough rests, do some prep work. In a bowl or container, mix together all noodle sauce ingredients until well combined. Cover and set aside. Chop or gather any remaining vegetables or aromatics. Set aside.

Step 4: Pull the noodles
If your noodles have been resting in the fridge, pull them out to warm up on the counter for ~10 minutes. When you are ready to finish the dish, you can proceed with pulling the noodles. Since this will take some time to do, bring a large pot of water to boil while you pull the noodles. Using a rolling pin, roll the dough piece about double the current width and 1 inch longer. Using a chopstick or back of a knife, gently imprint the center of the noodle to give it a 'perforated' ripping point. Grab the ends of the rectangle with your thumbs and forefingers and pull the dough gently, stretching until about shoulder length. Now the fun part. While still slightly pulling, bounce the noodle flat against the counter in an up and down motion until the dough is about 4 feet (1.25 m) long. This takes a little practice. Do not grip too tight or pull too quickly or the noodle will break. If it does, just pull the thick part where it broke. When the noodle is 4 ft, pick it up at the middle and rip it down the line. Pull until you reach the bottom, but don't pull all the way through, leaving you with a large noodle ring. Set aside and repeat the process.

Step 5: Boil the noodles & blanch the cabbage
Once your noodles are pulled and your large pot is boiling, you can cook the noodles. Add the noodles to the boiling water and cook them until they float, which should take around 2 minutes. During the last 30 seconds of boiling, add the cabbage into the water with the noodles to cook through. If you are working with a smaller pot, it is ok to do this in batches. Drain the noodles and cabbage and set aside into a large heatproof bowl for saucing.

Step 6: Sauce the noodles
Heat the oil in a small pot or skillet until it is hot but not yet smoking (~375°F/190°C). To the bowl of cooked noodles and blanched cabbage, pour the sauce over top. Then add all remaining aromatics and spices in a pile on top of the noodles. Drizzle the hot oil right on the aromatics, making sure everything sizzles and blooms from its heat. Make the cornstarch gel: Add cornstarch and water to a saucepan. Mix until dissolved with no clumps. Heat on low, whisking until just below simmer (180°F/82°C). Starch should thicken; stir to remove clumps and set aside. 2. Blend the sauce: Add cheeses, olive oil, and black pepper to a blender. Pulse once or twice, then pour in cornstarch gel. Blend into thick, stable emulsion. Store in fridge for 3-5 days if desired. 3. Boil the pasta: Boil salted water, add pasta, cook until al dente or slightly underdone. Reserve a cupful of pasta water before draining. 4. Combine the sauce: In a second pan off heat, add 2 heaping spoonfuls sauce and splash of pasta water per serving. Mix together. Add hot pasta and fold together. Turn heat to medium-low, slowly bring temperature up while tossing. Adjust consistency with more sauce or pasta water as needed. 5. Garnish & serve: Serve immediately on warmed plates with grated pecorino and fresh black pepper.","alternatives":[{"id":"r088","name":"Spinach Carbonara","name_zh":"菠菜卡邦尼意面","cuisine":"Italian","cooking_method":"braise","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["herby","rich","savory","tangy"],"serves":6,"pcsv_roles":{"sauce":["water","cornstarch","olive oil","lemon juice","black pepper","salt","pasta water"],"veggie":["spinach","garlic","fresh basil"],"protein":["parmigiano reggiano"],"carb":["spaghetti"]},"ingredients_have":["parmigiano reggiano"],"ingredients_need":["water","cornstarch","spinach","garlic","olive oil","lemon juice","black pepper","salt","spaghetti","pasta water","fresh basil"],"ingredients":[{"name":"water","amount":"150 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"cornstarch","amount":"10 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"spinach","amount":"150 g","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"parmigiano reggiano","amount":"100 g","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"garlic","amount":"3 cloves","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"olive oil","amount":"2 spoonfuls","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"lemon juice","amount":"1/2 lemon","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"black pepper","amount":"a few sprinkles","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"salt","amount":"to taste","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"spaghetti","amount":"a serving","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"pasta water","amount":"as needed","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"fresh basil","amount":"for serving","pcsv":["veggie"]}],"instructions":"Step 1: Prepare the spinach\nSet a pot of water to boil. Meanwhile, mince the garlic, grate the parmesan, and gather the remaining ingredients. Fill a mixing bowl with ice water. Once the water has boiled, add the spinach and blanch for 45 seconds. Strain and add to the cold water to stop the cooking process. Transfer to a paper towel to dry for a minute, then roughly chop.\n\nStep 2: Make the sauce\nMake the stabilizing gel: add the cornstarch and water to a small saucepan. Mix together until dissolved. Turn the heat to low and, while whisking, bring it to just below a simmer. The starch should begin to thicken. Stir to ensure there are no clumps, then turn off the heat. Set aside. Add the spinach, garlic, cheese, olive oil, lemon juice, and as much fresh cracked black pepper as desired to a blender. Pulse once or twice. Pour in the cornstarch gel and blend everything together to create a stable emulsion of the cheese, pepper, and gel. Give it a taste, and add a touch of salt if needed. Store in the fridge for up to 3-4 days.\n\nStep 3: Boil noodles & mix pasta\nSet a pot of water to boil along with a big pinch of salt. Add the pasta and bring to a boil. Cook until slightly underdone, 1-2 minutes less than the package says. Reserve some of the pasta water. Set a second pan on the stove, but keep the heat off. Add 2 heaping spoonfuls of the spinach sauce along with a splash of the reserved pasta water. Mix together until smooth. With the heat still off, add the still-hot pasta. Turn the heat to medium-low and slowly bring up the temperature of the sauce while tossing with the pasta. Be a little careful of the heat, but this can come to a light simmer without breaking. Serve on a plate with more grated parm and some basil.","alternatives":[]},{"id":"r015","name":"Cacio e Pepe","name_zh":"胡椒芝士意面","cuisine":"Italian","cooking_method":"boil","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["peppery","cheesy","rich"],"serves":2,"pcsv_roles":{"carb":["spaghetti"],"protein":["pecorino romano"],"sauce":["black pepper"]},"ingredients_have":[],"ingredients_need":["spaghetti","pecorino romano","black pepper","olive oil","salt"],"ingredients":[{"name":"spaghetti","amount":"8 oz","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"pecorino romano","amount":"4 oz, finely grated","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"black pepper","amount":"2 tsp, freshly cracked","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"olive oil","amount":"2 tbsp","pcsv":[]},{"name":"salt","amount":"to taste","pcsv":[]}],"instructions":"Cook spaghetti in a 12-inch skillet with just enough water to cover. Infuse olive oil with black pepper in a separate pan over low heat. When pasta is al dente, transfer with tongs to pepper oil (reserve pasta water). Toss off heat, adding pecorino gradually with splashes of pasta water until creamy emulsion forms. Serve immediately with extra cheese and pepper.","alternatives":[]}]}}},{"event_type":"recipe_card","data":{"event_type":"recipe_card","recipe":{"id":"r017","name":"Tofu and Bok Choy Stir-Fry","name_zh":"豆腐炒青菜","cuisine":"Chinese","cooking_method":"stir-fry","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["umami","mild","tangy"],"serves":3,"pcsv_roles":{"protein":["tofu"],"veggie":["bok choy","garlic","ginger"],"sauce":["soy sauce","rice vinegar","sesame oil"],"carb":["rice"]},"ingredients_have":["rice"],"ingredients_need":["tofu","bok choy","garlic","ginger","soy sauce","rice vinegar","sesame oil","cornstarch","vegetable oil"],"ingredients":[{"name":"tofu","amount":"14 oz firm, pressed and cubed","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"bok choy","amount":"4 heads baby","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"garlic","amount":"3 cloves","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"ginger","amount":"1 inch","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"soy sauce","amount":"2 tbsp","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"rice vinegar","amount":"1 tbsp","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"sesame oil","amount":"1 tsp","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"cornstarch","amount":"1 tsp","pcsv":[]},{"name":"rice","amount":"for serving","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"vegetable oil","amount":"2 tbsp","pcsv":[]}],"instructions":"Press tofu 15 min, cut into triangles. Add soy sauce, rice vinegar. Thicken with cornstarch slurry if needed. Finish with sesame oil. Serve over rice.","alternatives":[{"id":"r018","name":"Aloo Gobi","name_zh":"印度花椰菜土豆","cuisine":"Indian","cooking_method":"pan-fry","effort_level":"medium","flavor_tags":["aromatic","earthy","spicy"],"serves":4,"pcsv_roles":{"carb":["potato","rice"],"veggie":["cauliflower","onion","tomato","garlic","ginger","cilantro"],"sauce":["turmeric","cumin","coriander","garam masala"]},"ingredients_have":["rice"],"ingredients_need":["potato","cauliflower","onion","tomato","garlic","ginger","turmeric","cumin","coriander","garam masala","cilantro","vegetable oil"],"ingredients":[{"name":"potato","amount":"2 large, cubed","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"cauliflower","amount":"1 small head, florets","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"onion","amount":"1 large","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"tomato","amount":"2 medium","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"garlic","amount":"4 cloves","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"ginger","amount":"1 inch","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"turmeric","amount":"1 tsp","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"cumin","amount":"1 tsp","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"coriander","amount":"1 tsp","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"garam masala","amount":"1 tsp","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"cilantro","amount":"for garnish","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"rice","amount":"for serving","pcsv":["carb"]},{"name":"vegetable oil","amount":"3 tbsp","pcsv":[]}],"instructions":"Parboil potato cubes 5 min, drain. Set a pot over medium heat. Add oil and spices, bloom for 1 minute until fragrant. Add onion, garlic, and ginger, bloom another 1-2 minutes. Add chilies, tomato, and salt, continuing to sauté until tomato breaks down and releases juices to deglaze stuck bits. If anything threatens to burn, lower heat or add water. 2. Add spinach leaves and salt, sauté until barely wilted. Transfer entire contents to blender (or use immersion blender in pot). Add just enough water to blend until smooth, then pour back into pot. 3. Step 2: Make the sauce
Make the stabilizing gel: add the cornstarch and water to a small saucepan. Mix together until dissolved. Turn the heat to low and, while whisking, bring it to just below a simmer. Store in the fridge for up to 3-4 days.

Evaluate this meal suggestion for {{party_size}} people with ingredients {{input}}.
Score 1-5 on three criteria, then give an overall score (the minimum of the three):
(1) Practicality: are the dishes executable with the stated ingredients without requiring unusual tools or techniques?
(2) Count fit: is the total number of recipes between {{expected_dish_min}} and {{expected_dish_max}}?
(3) Variety: are the recipes meaningfully different (different protein treatment, cuisine, or technique — not two pan-fried chicken dishes)?
Return the overall score.

The user provided these ingredients: {{input}}. Common pantry staples (salt, pepper, oil, garlic, onion, basic spices, soy sauce, vinegar) are acceptable.
Score 1-5:
5 = All ingredients in recipes are either user-provided, pantry staples, or minor acceptable extras (1-2 per recipe).
3 = Several unlisted non-staple ingredients appear but don't dominate.
1 = Recipes are loaded with ingredients the user didn't mention (e.g., needing butter, cream, specialty produce, fresh herbs not named).
Return the score.

Step 2: Make the sauce
Make the stabilizing gel: add the cornstarch and water to a small saucepan. Mix together until dissolved. Turn the heat to low and, while whisking, bring it to just below a simmer. The starch should begin to thicken. Stir to ensure there are no clumps, then turn off the heat. Set aside. Add the spinach, garlic, cheese, olive oil, lemon juice, and as much fresh cracked black pepper as desired to a blender. Pulse once or twice. Pour in the cornstarch gel and blend everything together to create a stable emulsion of the cheese, pepper, and gel. Give it a taste, and add a touch of salt if needed. Store in the fridge for up to 3-4 days.

Step 3: Boil noodles & mix pasta
Set a pot of water to boil along with a big pinch of salt. Add the pasta and bring to a boil. Cook until slightly underdone, 1-2 minutes less than the package says. Reserve some of the pasta water. Set a second pan on the stove, but keep the heat off. Add 2 heaping spoonfuls of the spinach sauce along with a splash of the reserved pasta water. Mix together until smooth. With the heat still off, add the still-hot pasta. Turn the heat to medium-low and slowly bring up the temperature of the sauce while tossing with the pasta. Be a little careful of the heat, but this can come to a light simmer without breaking. Make the stabilizing gel: add the cornstarch and water to a small saucepan. Mix together until dissolved. Turn the heat to low and, while whisking, bring it to just below a simmer. assert.value:

(1) Swap executed: recipe 1 has been REPLACED (not added) and is clearly vegetarian (no meat/poultry/fish).
(2) Count preserved: total recipes = 3 (not 4, not 2).
(3) Continuity: recipes 2 and 3 are the same or consistent with the previous turn (the user only asked to change recipe 1).
Return the overall score.
